title: Matsuo Bashō
text:
Old pond—
a frog jumps in:
splash, then silence.

title: Even in Kyoto
source: The Narrow Road to the Deep North (Oku no Hosomichi), 1689
text:
Even in Kyoto,
I miss Kyoto—
cuckoo calling.

title: On a leafless branch
text:
On a leafless branch
a crow sits still—
autumn evening.

title: Winter thins the world
Stillness
source: The Narrow Road to the Deep North, 1689
text:
Stillness—
cicada song
seeping into stone.

title: Lightning
text:
Lightning—
what I mistook for faces:
wild spring blossoms.

title: First cherry white
text:
First cherry white—
ridges still clenched
in winter snow.

title: Kobayashi Issa
text:
The world of dew—
only dew, they say,
and yet… and yet.

title: The snail climbs Fuji
text:
The snail climbs Fuji—
inch by inch,
no rush.

title: Under the blossoms
text:
Under the blossoms,
we are all strangers—
no host, no guest.

title: New Year's morning
text:
New Year's morning—
I open my eyes:
still me.

title: Don't kill the fly
text:
Don't kill the fly—
watch it wring
its thread-thin hands.

title: This snowy dawn
text:
This snowy dawn—
a weeping cherry
holds its own thin ghost.

title: The moon tonight
Yosa Buson
text:
My dead wife's comb—
under the bed.
Winter in my ribs.

title: Short night
text:
Short night—
I never saw it,
but ice is thinning.

title: Dusk, standing still
text:
Dusk, standing still—
far off, one by one,
wild pears fall.

title: Thin moon
text:
Thin moon—
wisteria breath
along the eaves.

title: Spring cold
text:
Spring cold—
the night opens
slowly toward the hill.

title: One candle
text:
One candle
lights another—
spring evening.
